key_facts: Founded 2007; entered China distributed solar PPA in late 2014
First wholly foreign-owned enterprise (WFOE) to develop distributed solar business in China
Serves Fortune 500 multinationals: P&G, Danone, Nestlé, Volkswagen, Unilever
Zero-upfront rooftop solar PPA contracts — company self-finances the solar installation
EDF partnership (2016): joint venture for rooftop solar in China — 77MW+ confirmed deal
Model: multinational factory-owner signs 20-year PPA at 10–15% electricity discount vs grid tariff
ACC retains ownership of solar assets; earns from electricity sales minus installation/O&M costs

funding_history.event: EDF-ACC China JV for rooftop solar
funding_history.notes: EDF (French state utility) invested into JV; confirms international institutional interest in China C&I solar PPA model. 77MW announced as initial pipeline.

business_model_notes: China's first C&I rooftop solar PPA operator. Template for commercial-rooftop PPA that was subsequently copied by domestic players. Business logic: multinationals need verifiable renewable energy for Scope 2 reporting; factory rooftops are idle capital; ACC takes development risk in exchange for long-term contracted cashflows. First mover advantage in corporate sustainability buyer market, but vulnerable to domestic competition post-2016 when the model was well-understood.
competitive_position: Template innovator, not scale leader. Domestic competitors (Sungrow New Energy, Jinko Power) replicated PPA model at far greater scale with cheaper capital. ACC's competitive moat: foreign-brand trust for multinational procurement decisions + deep relationships with Fortune 500 sustainability teams.
research_notes: High confidence. EDF JV documented in PV-Tech 2016 article. ACC website and interviews confirm Fortune 500 customer list. Model well-documented in academic literature on China distributed solar finance.
